perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement, (i) the term “ Representative ” means, as to any person, such person’s affiliates and its and their respective directors, officers, general partners, managers, members, employees, agents and advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, legal counsel and accountants), and with the written consent (including via email, which such consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed) of the Company or as referenced on the attached Schedule A of this Agreement any consultants and potential financing sources
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement, (i) the term “ Associate ” means, as to any person, such person’s affiliates and its and their respective directors, officers, general partners, managers, members, employees, agents and advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, legal counsel and accountants), and with the written consent (including via email, which such consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed) of the Company or as referenced on the attached Schedule A of this Agreement any consultants and potential financing sources
perturbation.explanation: The term “Representative” is replaced with “Associate”. This creates an in-text contradiction because, while the definition seems similar, the subsequent use of 'Representative' throughout the document relies on the initial specific definition. By changing the defined term, it's now unclear whether the obligations imposed on 'Representatives' still apply, especially concerning confidentiality and use of Evaluation Material, leading to uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You and your Representatives shall use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of considering, evaluating and negotiating a Transaction, shall not use any of the Evaluation Material for any other purpose, shall keep the Evaluation Material strictly confidential, and, except as provided in this Section 2 and in Section 4 , shall not disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever without the prior written consent of the Company; provided, however, that the Evaluation Material may be disclosed to your Representatives who need to know the information so disclosed for the purpose of evaluating or advising you with respect to considering, evaluating and negotiating a Transaction, and who are directed by you to keep such Evaluation Material confidential and who are provided with a copy of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines "Representative", which is then used in the contradicted text to specify obligations regarding Evaluation Material. By changing the definition to "Associate", the contract now faces a contradiction: the contradicted text imposes specific duties on "Representatives", but the defined term has been altered. It's unclear if the obligations now apply to "Associates" instead, or if the original obligations on "Representatives" are nullified or still apply, creating ambiguity about who is bound by the confidentiality and usage restrictions of the Evaluation Material. This is a direct conflict and breaks compatibility, thus it is a clear and direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement, the term “ Evaluation Material ” means all information concerning the Company (whether furnished on or after the date hereof, whether prepared by the Company, its Representatives or otherwise, whether or not marked as being confidential, and irrespective of the form of communication, including oral as well as written and electronic communications) that is furnished to you or to your Representatives by or on behalf of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement, the term “ Data Provided ” means all information concerning the Company (whether furnished on or after the date hereof, whether prepared by the Company, its Representatives or otherwise, whether or not marked as being confidential, and irrespective of the form of communication, including oral as well as written and electronic communications) that is furnished to you or to your Representatives by or on behalf of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Evaluation Material' is replaced with 'Data Provided'. While both refer to information, 'Evaluation Material' has a specific implication of being used for assessment. Replacing it with 'Data Provided' makes the restrictions on usage less clear, as simply providing data doesn't inherently imply an obligation of strict confidentiality or limited use as defined later in the agreement. This contradiction weakens the enforceability of the confidentiality clauses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You and your Representatives shall use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of considering, evaluating and negotiating a Transaction, shall not use any of the Evaluation Material for any other purpose, shall keep the Evaluation Material strictly confidential, and, except as provided in this Section 2 and in Section 4 , shall not disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever without the prior written consent of the Company; provided, however, that the Evaluation Material may be disclosed to your Representatives who need to know the information so disclosed for the purpose of evaluating or advising you with respect to considering, evaluating and negotiating a Transaction, and who are directed by you to keep such Evaluation Material confidential and who are provided with a copy of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Evaluation Material' which the 'Contradicted Text' requires to be used 'solely for the purpose of considering, evaluating and negotiating a Transaction'. The changed text replaces 'Evaluation Material' with 'Data Provided'. The explanation states that 'Data Provided' lacks the specific implication of being used for assessment. This creates an in-text contradiction because the Contradicted Text now refers to 'Data Provided', but the term 'Data Provided' does not necessarily imply the same restricted use as 'Evaluation Material', therefore weakening enforceability and creating a contradiction. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology, where changes to terminology lead to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The furnishing to you or your Representatives of any Evaluation Material shall not be deemed to waive or in any manner diminish any attorney-client privilege, attorney work-product protection or other privilege or protection applicable to any such Evaluation Material.
perturbation.changed_text: The furnishing to you or your Representatives of any Data Provided shall not be deemed to waive or in any manner diminish any attorney-client privilege, attorney work-product protection or other privilege or protection applicable to any such Evaluation Material.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'Evaluation Material' with 'Data Provided' in Section 6, while keeping 'Evaluation Material' in the second half of the sentence, it creates contradiction. The first part of the sentence refers to 'Data Provided' while the second references 'Evaluation Material', creating uncertainty as to which type of information the privilege and protection apply to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Privileged Information . The furnishing to you or your Representatives of any Data Provided shall not be deemed to waive or in any manner diminish any attorney-client privilege, attorney work-product protection or other privilege or protection applicable to any such Evaluation Material. The parties hereto acknowledge and agree that, in connection with your consideration of the potential Transaction only and not for any other purpose, they (a) are or may become joint defendants in legal proceedings to which such Evaluation Material relates, and (b) intend that all such privileges and protections shall remain intact should either party become subject to any legal proceedings to which such Evaluation Material is relevant. In furtherance of the foregoing, each party hereto agrees not to claim or contend that the other party has waived any attorney-client privilege, attorney work-product protection or other protection or privilege by providing information pursuant to this Agreement or any subsequent agreement (definitive or otherwise) regarding a Transaction into which the parties hereafter may enter.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Evaluation Material' with 'Data Provided' but keeps 'Evaluation Material' later in the same sentence and also in the Contradicted Text. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition because it uses multiple terms without defining them in a consistent manner, creating conflicting obligations and uncertainty as to which information the privilege and protection apply to. The Contradicted Text uses both terms interchangeably further solidifying the contradiction. The change creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: You agree that, except as provided in a definitive agreement relating to a Transaction, for a period of one (1) year following the date of this Agreement, you shall not, and you shall not authorize, instruct, encourage or facilitate the ability of any of your Representatives and any person acting on behalf of to, in any manner, directly or indirectly, solicit for hire any of the officers or employees of the Company with whom you had contact or first became aware of during the process contemplated herein
perturbation.changed_text: You agree that, except as provided in a definitive agreement relating to a Transaction, for a period of one (1) year following the date of this Agreement, you shall not, and you shall not authorize, instruct, encourage or facilitate the ability of any of your Associates and any person acting on behalf of to, in any manner, directly or indirectly, solicit for hire any of the officers or employees of the Company with whom you had contact or first became aware of during the process contemplated herein
perturbation.explanation: Since we redefined 'Representative' as 'Associate' in the document's intro, changing it here creates conflict as the definition is unclear and can be argued what it means in this context. Therefore, It weakens the non-solicitation clause by creating ambiguity about who is bound by it since it contradicts what was said in the intro. This shift weakens enforceability regarding the non-solicitation of the company's officers and employees.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Solicitation . You agree that, except as provided in a definitive agreement relating to a Transaction, for a period of one (1) year following the date of this Agreement, you shall not, and you shall not authorize, instruct, encourage or facilitate the ability of any of your Associates and any person acting on behalf of to, in any manner, directly or indirectly, solicit for hire any of the officers or employees of the Company with whom you had contact or first became aware of during the process contemplated herein; provided, however, that the foregoing does not preclude you or your Representatives from: (a) soliciting employees through, or hiring employees who respond to, general job advertisements or similar notices that are not targeted specifically at the employees of the Company; or (b) soliciting or hiring employees whose employment has been terminated by the Company.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Representatives' to 'Associates' in the non-solicitation clause introduces ambiguity, as it contradicts the document's definition of 'Representative' and 'Associate.' The change in terminology creates uncertainty about who is bound by the non-solicitation agreement, directly conflicting with the contradicted text where the obligation is stated, thus leading to a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties hereto concerning the confidentiality of Transaction Information and Evaluation Material, the standstill agreements, the no-solicitation agreement and the other covenants, undertakings and subject matter set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties hereto concerning the confidentiality of Transaction Information and Data Provided, the standstill agreements, the no-solicitation agreement and the other covenants, undertakings and subject matter set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Evaluation Material' to 'Data Provided' here creates confusion as to which information is covered under the entire agreement. Given that 'Evaluation Material' has already appeared previously in the text, as well as 'Data Provided' in others, it is no longer clear if all clauses relating to the previous 'Evaluation Material', apply to the term 'Data Provided'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (d) Entire Agreement . This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties hereto concerning the confidentiality of Transaction Information and Data Provided, the standstill agreements, the no-solicitation agreement and the other covenants, undertakings and subject matter set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Evaluation Material' while the changed text replaces this with 'Data Provided'. The explanation states this substitution creates confusion, potentially excluding previously included information ('Evaluation Material') from the agreement, even though both terms are used in the document. The 'Contradicted Text' uses the new term, 'Data Provided', thus the question is whether the substitution created a contradiction. Given that Evaluation Material was already referenced elsewhere, it is now not clear what is included and excluded by Data Provided. This meets the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology' which creates an in-text contradiction due to the interchangeable use of undefined terms. This creates uncertainty.
